item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as they were taken by the author of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, but one that I hope you take and then do some editing with after. I think the actual scp itself is fine, and the story too is fine. But your interviews are really poor, both in writing and in grammar. If you fixed them up, I think this scp would be just fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with Shaolin. I'm a tentative +1, but I'd love to see what happens next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I created an account just so I can upvote this. It deserves better than zero.

The tone is a little underwhelming, but the concept of a house you cannot enter even with drones and military vehicles, all because of eternally vigilant guard dogs is intriguing to me.

My one complaint though is why would the Foundation mind wipe the cleaning lady and just let her go? She is literally the one person who can safely enter the property and interact with the dogs. Why would they just let her go instead of giving her a camera and make her explore the house?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I shifted around the paragraphs a bit, hopefully that makes it less confusing.

Thanks for your feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, upvoting. This actually isn't too bad. I mean, the idea is rather simple, but you leave an important question unanswered, which entices the reader: where did they come from?
You should move the paragraphs describing SCP-3724-1 and -2 to the top of your description, because I was reading the description of the active state, and I had NO CLUE what was going on.

item.title: Incident Report
item.content:encoded: Quick note that your incident report is still written with an "XXXX" instead of the SCP's number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main problem with this is that the story never goes anywhere. You have dogs in a house that attack you, but why? You have a girl that died in a car crash, but it's never stated how this is related to the anomaly. A mysterious girl breaks into the house, but it's just the housekeeper.

These are all prompts that you could've used to expand your SCP and make it so it doesn't feel anticlimatic. For now, though, it just feels like the beginning of something good. Try to further explain the cause and motives of the anomalies involved.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: these pics are like 2mb apiece could you shrink em down a bit
